user may acquire AT MOST two stores, ever:      - a `LabelStore`, deployed via `deployLabelStoreFor` by a protocol-registered caller        during registration; and      - a `UserStore`, claimed via `claimUserStore` by the user themselves.      Both are `BeaconProxy` instances pointing at their respective `UpgradeableBeacon`.      The factory owns both beacons so the factory owner can upgrade implementations for      every proxy atomically. Neither per-user mapping is ever transferred, reassigned, or      overwritten after the first write; bindings are permanent.","errors":{"AlreadyDeployed(address,address)":[{"params":{"existingStore":"The already-deployed store address.","user":"The user for whom the store exists."}}],"InvalidImplementation(address)":[{"params":{"implementation":"The invalid implementation argument."}}],"InvalidProtocolRegistry(address)":[{"params":{"protocolRegistry":"The invalid registry argument."}}],"InvalidUser(address)":[{"params":{"user":"The invalid user argument."}}],"NotAuthorised(address)":[{"params":{"caller":"The unauthorised msg.sender."}}],"OwnableInvalidOwner(address)":[{"details":"The owner is not a valid owner account. claimUserStore().details: Self-claim only; `_owner` on the resulting store is always `msg.sender`,      regardless of who pays gas. One store per caller, forever.

constructor.details: A single `new StoreFactory(protocolRegistry)` call wires everything:      - Deploys a fresh `LabelStore` implementation.      - Deploys a fresh `UserStore` implementation.      - Constructs both `UpgradeableBeacon` instances, owned by `address(this)`        so `upgrade*Implementation` can delegate to `beacon.upgradeTo`.      Keeping the implementation deployments inside the constructor removes a      class of operator error: there is no "did I deploy the implementation first?" step      and no way to pass the wrong implementation address.Implementations and beacons are deployed inline so a single factory address fully      describes the store topology, removing a class of operator error around mismatched      beacons.

params.protocolRegistry_: The protocol registry for writer auth on label stores.

deployLabelStoreFor(address).details: Callable by the factory owner OR any address currently registered in the protocol

params.user: The user the store is bound to forever.

returns.store: The bound store address, or zero.

getLabelStores(uint256,uint256).details: Insertion order of `deployLabelStoreFor` calls. `offset >= getLabelStoreCount()`      returns an empty array; result length is `min(limit, count - offset)`.

returns.store: The bound store address, or zero.

getUserStores(uint256,uint256).details: Insertion order of `claimUserStore` calls. `offset >= getUserStoreCount()`      returns an empty array; result length is `min(limit, count - offset)`.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

upgradeLabelStoreImplementation(address).details: Callable by the factory owner only. Delegates to `UpgradeableBeacon.upgradeTo`.

upgradeUserStoreImplementation(address).details: Callable by the factory owner only. Delegates to `UpgradeableBeacon.upgradeTo`.

constructor.notice: Deploys the factory together with both store implementations and beacons.

getLabelStore(address).notice: Returns the `LabelStore` address bound to `user`, or the zero address if none.

getLabelStoreCount().notice: Returns the total number of `LabelStore` proxies ever deployed.

getLabelStores(uint256,uint256).notice: Paginated enumeration over every `LabelStore` proxy ever deployed.

getUserStore(address).notice: Returns the `UserStore` address bound to `user`, or the zero address if none.

getUserStoreCount().notice: Returns the total number of `UserStore` proxies ever claimed.

getUserStores(uint256,uint256).notice: Paginated enumeration over every `UserStore` proxy ever claimed.

protocolRegistry().notice: Protocol registry used to authorise `deployLabelStoreFor` callers.

upgradeLabelStoreImplementation(address).notice: Upgrades the `LabelStore` implementation for every existing and future proxy.

upgradeUserStoreImplementation(address).notice: Upgrades the `UserStore` implementation for every existing and future proxy.

documentation.text:
@notice Beacon backing every `LabelStore` proxy.
 @dev Public getter name is interface-constrained by {IStoreFactory}.

@notice Beacon backing every `UserStore` proxy.

@notice Returns implementation version.

@notice Internal authorisation check deferred from the `onlyOwnerOrProtocol` modifier.

@title StoreFactory
 @notice Factory for the two per-user DotNS store types, sharing one factory contract and two
 beacons. @dev Each user may acquire AT MOST two stores, ever:
      - a `LabelStore`, deployed via `deployLabelStoreFor` by a protocol-registered caller
        during registration; and
      - a `UserStore`, claimed via `claimUserStore` by the user themselves.
      Both are `BeaconProxy` instances pointing at their respective `UpgradeableBeacon`.
      The factory owns both beacons so the factory owner can upgrade implementations for
      every proxy atomically. Neither per-user mapping is ever transferred, reassigned, or
      overwritten after the first write; bindings are permanent.
 @custom:security-contact admin@parity.io
